commit.message:
MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2: reconcile A2 docs + stale menu rustdoc + lint-doc counts

Docs/comments-only post-audit cleanup (0 source-logic, 0 routing, 0 Cargo.toml).
Output of the 2026-06-02 deep read-only audit: the living docs froze at the #292
(A1) reconcile and predated A2 (#295), plus stale menu rustdoc and stale
ARCHITECTURE_LINTS counts. All numbers below were VERIFIED against source on
disk (anchored greps / wc) before editing.

State-doc A2 reconcile (forward-only, the #292 pattern -- prior snapshots
preserved byte-identical):
- Status.md + HANDOFF.md: prepend a new 2026-06-02 A2 snapshot recording the
  Edit menu (Undo/Redo) landed in #295 via MenuRegistry; supersedes the A1
  snapshot's "Edit menu deferred" claim. View/Play STAY deferred.
- plans/BASELINE.md: prepend a new "### 2026-06-02 -- Edit menu now
  registry-produced (#295 A2)" subsection above the A1 one. The A1 subsection
  (incl. its Edit/View/Play still-open bullet) is LEFT byte-identical as dated
  history, superseded forward -- per the doc's own forward-only convention
  (which is why #292 likewise left the older #287/#288 line untouched).
- change.md: append one forward-only A2 entry.

Stale menu rustdoc (crates/editor-egui-host/src/{lib.rs,handoff.rs}):
- Remove the false "Dispatch A only ENQUEUES -- nothing drains the queue yet" /
  "lands in Dispatch B" (the drain landed in #288 -- drain_and_route_menu_commands).
- Widen "the File menu bar enqueues" -> "the File + Edit menu bars enqueue"
  (Edit also enqueues post-A2).

docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md -- complete count reconcile vs source:
- forbidden-dep rule count 6 -> 7 at every LIVE spot (§2 table, §refs "7-rule
  taxonomy", source-files row): Rule 7 (rge-editor-shell stays loader-free --
  cannot dep rge-scene-loader/rge-data) landed #280; forbidden_dep.rs module-doc
  says "seven dependency rules". The HISTORICAL "rules 3-6 were dead code"
  references (audit-1/-5/-6 prefix-mismatch narrative, lines 14/47/165/167/169/
  235) are LEFT verbatim -- Rule 7 postdates that episode, so "3-6" is correct
  there; bumping them to 3-7 would falsify the history.
- forbidden_dep.rs line/test counts: 699L -> 817L; 22 inline tests -> 27
  (grep -c '#[test]' = 27). Inline-test total 38 -> 43; overall test total
  107 -> 112 (43 inline + 69 integration).
- exemptions.toml count 60 -> 28 at every spot (Status row, Reference-impls row,
  §4 registry, graph-foundation section, source-files row): the file has exactly
  28 [[exemption]] blocks (anchored grep -c '^[[exemption]]'), ALL
  lint="failure-class" -- NO graph-foundation entry, NO reserved entry. The old
  "60 = 1 graph-foundation FP + 58 failure-class + 1 reserved" breakdown is
  fully gone. The dated §"audit findings" row that recorded the old 60-count is
  preserved as a dated finding + annotated "since reduced to 28".

NB / self-correction: an earlier draft of this commit miscounted the exemptions
as "29 = 1 graph-foundation + 28 failure-class" -- that came from an UNANCHORED
grep matching the file's schema comment (`# Each [[exemption]] ...`) plus
trusting the doc's stale graph-foundation claim. The anchored count is 28, all
failure-class; corrected throughout.

Out of scope (not touched): the dated 2026-05-09 "failure-class enforcement
progress" snapshot (§9) and the dated audit-findings row are preserved as
historical; other unflagged numbers; feature code; routing; Cargo.toml.

Verification: ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md + change.md are doc-only (no compile/lint
impact); the .rs rustdoc edits are byte-identical to the prior gate-passed
commit (local gate VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 lints); every changed number
re-verified against source via anchored grep / wc; git diff --check clean;
0 Cargo.toml. CI re-runs the full gate on this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2: correct Play-deferred framing (Codex review Finding 1)

Codex review (NEEDS_CORRECTION, 2026-06-02_23-42-14) raised two findings on #296.
This commit resolves Finding 1; Finding 2 was already resolved by the prior amend
(067138d), which Codex had not yet seen (it reviewed the pre-amend EXEC 22-30-16).

Finding 1 -- the new A2 forward snapshots wrongly said the Play menu "needs a new
action first", lumping it with View. That is false and would reintroduce the exact
Layer-1 drift the 2026-06-02 deep audit caught. Source shows Play is ALREADY
runtime-wired:
- crates/editor-ui/src/menus/command.rs:64-71 -- Command::PlayStart / PlayStop /
  PlayPause / PlayStep variants all exist.
- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1576 -- EditorShell::handle_button drives
  the Play/Pause/Stop/Step arms (PlayState, snapshots, audit, game ticks); the
  Phase 5.3 PIE round-trip is CLOSED (plans/BASELINE.md:718).
So the Play menu needs ONLY menu/UI wiring (FIFO-drain -> handle_button), NOT a new
action -- it is the intended next feature beat as pure wiring. View -> Reset Camera
is the one that genuinely needs a new action (a camera-reset via
isometric_camera_for_bounds).

Reframed in all four live A2 snapshots -- Status.md, HANDOFF.md, plans/BASELINE.md,
change.md: View needs a new action; Play needs only wiring (no new action), and is
next. (BASELINE.md additionally notes Play satisfies the A2 "Command variant + a
reachable shell action" criterion via handle_button; it was simply out of A2's
scope.)

Finding 2 (ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md count reconcile incomplete + 29-vs-28) was already
fully resolved by amend 067138d: exemptions = 28, all failure-class, no
graph-foundation or reserved (anchored grep -c '^[[exemption]]' = 28; lint kinds =
failure-class only); forbidden-dep = 7 rules at every live spot; no stale 6-rule /
60-entry references remain (only the dated, annotated audit-findings row). Verified
again at HEAD this round.

Docs-only; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing change. git diff --check
clean; 4 files; 0 Cargo.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2: fix stale architecture-lint counts — test totals + line counts (Codex re-review)

Codex re-review confirmed the two original findings resolved at 01a0671 and raised
a third source-verifiable stale count in docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md: the
architecture-lint TEST TOTALS had drifted (the editor-state-ownership integration
suite grew 7 -> 16 since the doc's "69 integration" was written; 69 + 9 = 78). A
proactive full cross-check of the same count-class then found stale per-lint LINE
counts too (the same suite growth dragged editor_state_ownership.rs 353L -> 500L).
This commit reconciles BOTH against source so the doc's "complete count reconcile"
claim actually holds.

Test totals -- source truth at HEAD (grep -coE '#[test]'):
- inline 43 (forbidden_dep 27 + split_exemption 6 + failure_class 5 +
  snapshot_participate 5)
- integration 78 (command_bus 7 + editor_state_ownership 16 + failure_class 7 +
  forbidden_dep 6 + graph_foundation 9 + kernel_isolation 8 + no_utils 7 +
  projection_modules 8 + snapshot_participate 5 + split_exemption 5)
- total 121
Fixed every LIVE spot to 121 / 78 / 16 (:5, :20, :66, :133, :142, :144, :147) +
the authoring-time-note forward-pointer (:228 "107-test" -> "121-test"). All 10
per-lint headers cross-checked; only editor-state-ownership was stale.

Line counts -- source truth at HEAD (wc -l), 5 occurrences fixed:
- main.rs                 108L -> 121L   (:8)
- editor_state_ownership  353L -> 500L   (:66 header, :259 source-files row)
- snapshot_participate     378L -> 377L   (:96 header, :264 source-files row)
The other 9 cited L values already matched wc -l (817/250/168/46/316/318/308/160/
239); full 12-file cross-check now passes.

PRESERVED as dated history (NOT source-current): :231 authoring-time reconciliation
("97 total = 33 inline + 64 integration"; Status.md's separate "| 69" crate tally)
-- explicitly "as this doc was first authored", not a current-source claim.

Docs-only; 1 file; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing change.
git diff --check clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
